codeReview.summary: The feature lands most of the requested search machinery, but two contract-level behaviors are still wrong: LMR is only enabled from depth 5 instead of depth 3, and the aspiration-window retry logic always falls through to a full-window re-search after the widened retry. There is also a smaller null-move restriction that disables pruning in many non-KP endgames beyond what the feature requested.

issues.description: Late-move reductions are gated behind `depth >= 5`, but the feature contract explicitly required reductions for late moves starting at depth >= 3. As a result, depth-3 and depth-4 searches never apply LMR, so this implementation does not actually deliver the requested behavior.

The aspiration-window implementation does not match the requested search flow: it only starts once `depth >= 5` instead of after the first completed iteration, and after a narrow-window fail it always performs a full-window re-search immediately after the +/-200 retry (`lines 751-757`), even when the widened retry succeeded. That defeats the intended 'widen to +/-200, then fall back to full window on fail' behavior and throws away much of the expected node-reduction benefit.

Null-move pruning is additionally disabled unless `search_total_non_king_pieces(pos) > 3`. The feature description only carved out KP endgames / positions without non-pawn material, so this extra guard shuts null-move pruning off in many simple but non-pawn endgames (for example KQK or KRK), making the optimization materially narrower than requested.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `engine-worker` skill is out of sync with the repository layout and validator commands. It still tells workers to edit `engine/` and `tests/` files and compile `engine/*.c tests/*.c`, while mission guidance and the actual repo use `engine/src/*.c` and `engine/tests/*.c`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/engine-worker/SKILL.md:35-69` references `engine/` + `tests/` and `make test`; `/Users/aravhawk/.factory/missions/70ca98e6-cb3b-4e1c-9eb9-4d887ed17139/AGENTS.md:39-58` documents `engine/src/`, `engine/tests/`, and the gcc command the worker actually used in the transcript skeleton.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission state did not make it clear that frontend package scripts might be absent during engine-only milestones, so the worker had to harden `.factory/services.yaml` mid-feature just to let lint/typecheck validators no-op cleanly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `2026-03-31T05-15-08-683Z__engine-pruning-extensions__f4f3d37b-ea6e-440c-888c-2e094f626405.json` records a `skillFeedback` deviation for final validation, and commit `c29ea62` changes `.factory/services.yaml` to guard `pnpm typecheck` / `pnpm lint` behind package-script existence checks.

summary: Reviewed commit `c29ea62` and the worker transcript for `engine-pruning-extensions`. The feature should not pass scrutiny yet: LMR and aspiration windows both miss required behavior from the feature contract, although the added null-move/stalemate coverage and repo-state observations were reviewed successfully.
